steps.title: Fastest check: compare values in a table
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $-8x^{6}+6x^{3}-10x+12$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Subtract each term inside the parentheses:
$6x^3 - 4x + 7 - (8x^6 + 6x - 5) = 6x^3 - 4x + 7 - 8x^6 - 6x + 5$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-2x^{2}-10x-12$: Wrong leading degree ($x^2$ instead of $x^6$) and wrong sign on the constant. ✗
  • Choice B — $-8x^{6}+6x^{3}-10x+12$: Matches the simplified result exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — $-8x^{6}+6x^{3}+2x+2$: Wrong $x$ coefficient ($+2x$ instead of $-10x$) and wrong constant. ✗
  • Choice D — $-2x^{9}-2x+2$: Wrong leading degree and wrong coefficients throughout. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
